facts.value: etching tool designed for fine detail work. A precise tool will help you avoid damaging the delicate parts.
setting adhesive like CA (cyanoacrylate) glue. Apply it sparingly to avoid overflow and damage to surrounding details.
drill holes**: Pre-drill small holes where you plan to attach wire details. This will make attachment easier and more precise.

facts.predicate: Study detailed reference images and drawings of the B
facts.value: 29 to understand the rigging configuration. Create a simple rigging guide or diagram to follow during assembly.
29 has multiple gun turrets with intricate details. Photo-etching sets for the B-29 often include PE parts for these turrets. Take your time with these delicate assemblies.
29 has several antennas and rigging details. Fine wire (0.3mm or smaller) will work best for these features.

facts.value: 29 project! It's a complex and rewarding build, and mastering photo-etching and wire details on this model will significantly advance your modeling skills. Feel free to reach out if you have any specific questions as you progress with the build!

Thanks for the tips! One more thing – I'm interested in exploring scale modeling as a hobby long-term. I'm thinking about budgeting around $500-600 per year to spend on kits, tools, and supplies. I'm also considering joining a local modeling club that meets every second Thursday to connect with other enthusiasts. I'm based in New York.
what would be a realistic timeline for becoming proficient with advanced techniques like photo-etching and wire details?
